purpose: Defines the `!cancel-safe [dashboardId]` command for graceful swarm shutdown. Sets an internal master flag to prevent further dispatches, polls progress files every 10 seconds waiting for in-progress agents to finish naturally, then marks remaining never-dispatched pending tasks as failed/cancelled. Preserves all completed and naturally-finishing work. Produces logs.json entries for shutdown initiation, per-agent finishes, and final completion summary, plus progress files for cancelled-pending tasks.
gotchas: This is one of the rare exceptions where the master writes progress files (only for never-dispatched pending tasks)
10-minute timeout warning prompts user to either keep waiting or escalate to `!cancel --force`
Completed and failed progress files are LEFT ALONE — never overwritten
The graceful shutdown flag lives in the master's working memory only — it does not persist across master sessions
Polling cadence is 10 seconds — not configurable; aggressive polling would conflict with worker writes
patterns: 11-step procedure: parse → read init → read progress → set flag → log initiation → display message → poll loop → mark cancelled-pending → update master task → log completion → display final report
Three-state classification of agents at finalization: completed (preserve), failed during execution (preserve), never-dispatched (write cancelled progress file)
Status emoji used in summary tables (Completed/Failed/Cancelled)
conventions: Contrasts itself explicitly with `!cancel` in a callout block
Numbered Steps with embedded JSON code blocks for log/progress file shapes
Final markdown report template with three result tables (Completed/Cancelled/Failed)
